title: Vitest + targeted property tests
acceptance_criteria: vitest.config.ts defines two projects: 'unit' (include: src/**/*.test.ts, exclude: src/test/integration/**) and 'integration' (include: src/test/integration/**); sets globals: true, environment: 'node'
package.json scripts updated: test:unit uses 'vitest run --project unit', test:integration uses 'vitest run --project integration', test:watch uses 'vitest --project unit'
package.json adds devDependencies: vitest, @vitest/coverage-v8, fast-check, decimal.js

src/utils/pnl.ts exports: calculatePnl(entryStraddleValue: string, exitStraddleValue: string, lots: number, lotSize: number): { grossPnl: string, isProfit: boolean } — uses decimal.js; position is SHORT so profit = entry > exit

pnl.property.test.ts: CRITICAL test — property test using fast-check: for any entry=100, exit=130 → grossPnl is negative (loss); for entry=100, exit=70 → grossPnl is positive (profit). Also tests: pnl of 100 × ₹0.10 increments = exactly ₹10.00 (no float drift vs decimal.js oracle); accumulation of 1000 random decimal P&L values matches decimal.js sum

triggers.property.test.ts: property tests for hard SL math (fires at exactly entry*(1+sl_pct), not at entry*(1+sl_pct-ε)), trailing SL ratchet (TSL only moves down, never up; fires at correct threshold), profit target math, daily loss cap accumulation — all using decimal.js as oracle

atm-strike.property.test.ts: property tests for getAtmStrike (imported stub/placeholder that will be implemented in T-11): NIFTY strikes always multiples of 50; BankNifty always multiples of 100; Sensex always multiples of 100; result is the nearest valid strike to spot price
Coverage tracked (HTML report output to coverage/) but no threshold gate — vitest --coverage generates the report
All property tests pass on first run with bun test:unit
output_format: code plus plain English explanation of every non-obvious decision
